description: Synthetic user scenarios for the Aid Navigator demo. No real user data is used, per hackathon guidelines.

personas.name: Maria
personas.narrative: First-generation college student, family of 4, parents' combined income ~$42,000/year. Starting her first year at a public 4-year university and worried she can't afford it.

personas.name: Devon
personas.narrative: Sophomore at a public university, household AGI ~$85,000, family of 4. Wants to know if work-study or loans could help cover the gap his Pell Grant doesn't.

personas.name: Priya
personas.narrative: Independent student (24, supports herself), works part-time, attending community college, AGI ~$18,000/year.

personas.name: The Alvarez family
personas.narrative: Two siblings in college at the same time, family of 5, household AGI ~$70,000. Want to understand how having two kids in school at once changes their aid.

personas.name: James
personas.narrative: Household receives SNAP benefits; family of 3, AGI ~$28,000. Unsure if that affects his aid eligibility -- demonstrates the Auto-Zero pathway.

personas.name: Sofia
personas.narrative: Higher-income family (AGI ~$160,000, family of 4) -- demonstrates the 'may not qualify for Pell, but still gets the unsubsidized loan info' case so the tool doesn't feel useless to higher earners.
